result.text:
NEW — Republicans on Capitol Hill are coming to grips with an emboldened President Trump who is determined to flex his power over the GOP, at seemingly any cost.

“It seems like he’s given up on holding the majority and focusing on loyalty in the minority,” one House Republican said.

The president, fresh off crushing a series of GOP foes in primaries, even openly threatened Rep. Brian Fitzpatrick today — a key swing district Republican Speaker Mike Johnson and GOP leaders quickly tried to defend from Trump’s attacks. 

“Look, Brian Fitzpatrick has a very difficult district — he has an independent streak, as we all know — but he’s a very close friend and colleague of mine,” Johnson told @politico, adding that he was “working hard to get him reelected.”

Rep. Richard Hudson of North Carolina, who leads the House GOP campaign arm, noted that “holding that seat is really important for holding the majority.”

More w/ @jordainc @MyahWard 

https://t.co/KyRDZIuKN7 via @politico

result.text:
I'm thrilled and deeply honored to announce my selection as a 2026-2027 research fellow at @TheWilsonCenter. I'm joining an outstanding group of scholars, researchers, and leading experts in their fields to produce research and analysis that informs Members of Congress and their staff about some of the world's most pressing issues. It's truly humbling to consider the cohort of fellows I'll be joining this year.

My research will examine the Tibetan resistance movement, from the Cold War to today, and analyze the landscape of competing visions about the movement’s future. I'll also look at how Tibet continues to shape geopolitical flashpoints along China's Himalayan frontier. My work will help reframe Tibet as a strategic security issue that demands American attention.

Excited to get to work!

https://t.co/3jPhYwvGip

(Photo is of Tsering Dhondup, a Cold War-era Tibetan freedom fighter, at his home in India's Ladakh Region in 2023.)

I was the only person in my family who voted for Zelenskyy. And because of it, I ended up in a bitter fight with my mother at Easter. What should have been a family gathering turned into a political battlefield.

“Army. Language. Faith” will destroy Ukraine, I argued with my relatives. I warned that toxic ethnic nationalism, fused with militarization and hysterical Russophobia, would deepen authoritarianism, normalize censorship and repression, and lock the country into a permanent state of war.

Back then, however, I associated all of this with Poroshenko and his camp - not with Zelenskyy. 
Not with the Russian-speaking, secular Jew who promised he would “kneel before Putin” if that was what it took to end the war. 

Not with the man for whom Bandera was no hero, and the OUN-UPA were those who had “shot Red Army soldiers in the back.”

I could not have imagined, even in my darkest nightmare, that under President Zelenskyy, neo-Nazis would penetrate virtually every sector of the defense and security apparatus; that Nazi collaborators would be glorified at the state level; that entire military regiments would bear their names.

I never believed that the Azov movement would evolve from a marginal fringe into two full military corps, or that men once involved in pogroms against Roma and Vietnamese communities would receive high military ranks and positions of power.

Most of all, I never imagined that I - a Jewish woman and a Holocaust scholar - would become a target of neo-Nazis directed by the security services of a state whose president is himself Jewish.

Today is a day of reckoning for my political choices. I find myself reflecting on what, if anything, I can still do to help undo their catastrophic consequences.

This ridiculous campaign feels orchestrated and its audience is likely Trump’s administration or Trump himself. The hope is to sway them, bring them “to the right side of history”. 

But the problem is that it is completely ungrounded in reality. It’s not a turning point in war. Ukraine is not beginning to win. It’s barely managing to hold the front while preventing a social and infrastructural collapse. 

Decisions premised on delusions about a turning tide in Ukraine could lead to far more disastrous consequences than the war in Iran. Meaning self-damage primarily, provided it doesn’t result in a nuclear conflict.

result.text: Putin’s paranoia is one sign that the war has now entered a fourth stage, one that is far more favorable to Ukraine. It is far from the only sign. Despite an intense recruiting effort, Russia is only able to replace about 70 percent of those lost. The strain is so difficult that the new recruits are thrown into the field with little training. They are sent into a charnel house to be counted among the next month’s 30,000.

result.text:
Today, we share a breakthrough on the planar unit distance problem, a famous open question first posed by Paul Erdős in 1946.

For nearly 80 years, mathematicians believed the best possible solutions looked roughly like square grids.

An OpenAI model has now disproved that belief, discovering an entirely new family of constructions that performs better.

This marks the first time AI has autonomously solved a prominent open problem central to a field of mathematics.

result.text:
Another drone. Another air alert in the Baltics. This time - near Lithuania’s border with Belarus. #NATO Baltic Air Policing was activated.

These drone incidents don’t exist in isolation. The growing wave of drone violations across the Baltic states is a direct consequence of Russia’s war against #Ukraine and #Belarus’ complicity in it. Moscow and Minsk are responsible for breaches of our airspace and threats to civilian safety.

Meanwhile, Russia continues its information offensive - falsely accusing Baltic states of enabling drone strikes against Russia. Lithuania has never allowed its territory or airspace to be used for such attacks.

The NATO& EU response cannot stay symbolic:
-NATO’s relevant capabilities - Eastern Sentry and Baltic Sentry missions - must be reinforced 
- EU Eastern Flank Watch initiative must be implemented& Special EU instrument for resilience of Eastern Flank urgently created - NATO’s frontline needs air defense& stronger counter-drone capabilities - EU critical infrastructure protection must be strengthened, as well as measures to counter Kremlin’s propaganda narratives

Raul Castro is 94, and he has spent a majority of those years committing innumerable crimes against the people of Cuba. I've talked with many Cuban Floridians, and what the Cuban people want is clear: regime change, freedom, and a fresh start. My family fled communism when I was 4 years old. I hope Raul Castro rots in jail.

Our leaders in Washington must think boldly and clearly about ending this regime while remaining skeptical of U.S. military force, as it has proven unsuccessful in securing American interests and a functioning democracy in the past. Freedom for Cuba demands a real plan, not an open-ended show of force with no clear endgame.

data.translation:
Our President, H.E. Recep Tayyip Erdoğan, held a phone call with U.S. President Donald Trump.

The leaders discussed bilateral relations between Türkiye and the United States, as well as regional and global issues.

In the call, our President expressed that he views the decision to extend the ceasefire in our region's conflict process as a positive development, that he believes a reasonable solution is possible on contentious issues, and that our support for the constructive initiatives put forward will continue.

President Erdoğan stated that the establishment of a stable environment in Syria would be an important gain for our entire region, that Türkiye's support for Syria will continue uninterrupted, and that steps must be taken to prevent the situation in Lebanon from deteriorating further.

Our President noted that preparations for the NATO Ankara Summit are ongoing and that we are exerting maximum effort to ensure the Summit is a success in every respect.

During the call, President Erdoğan conveyed his condolences to U.S. President Trump over the heinous attack on a mosque in San Diego, California, two days prior, while stating that Türkiye stands against such hate crimes regardless of the religious group targeted.

result.text:
Cumhurbaşkanımız Sayın Recep Tayyip Erdoğan, ABD Başkanı Donald Trump ile bir telefon görüşmesi gerçekleştirdi.

Liderler, Türkiye-ABD ikili ilişkileri ile bölgesel ve küresel konuları ele aldı.

Cumhurbaşkanımız görüşmede, bölgemizdeki çatışma sürecinde ateşkesin uzatılması kararını müspet bir gelişme olarak gördüğünü, ihtilaflı konularda makul bir çözümün mümkün olduğuna inandığını, ortaya konan yapıcı girişimlere desteğimizin süreceğini ifade etti.

Cumhurbaşkanı Erdoğan, Suriye’de istikrar ortamının kök salmasının tüm bölgemiz için önemli bir kazanım olduğunu, Türkiye’nin Suriye’ye yönelik desteğinin aralıksız süreceğini, ayrıca Lübnan’daki durumun kötüleşmesinin önüne geçilmesi gerektiğini belirtti.

Cumhurbaşkanımız, NATO Ankara Zirvesi hazırlıklarının sürdüğünü, Zirve’nin her açıdan başarılı geçmesi amacıyla azami gayret gösterdiğimizi ifade etti. 

Cumhurbaşkanı Erdoğan görüşmede, ABD Başkanı Trump’a önceki gün ABD’nin San Diego kentindeki bir camiye düzenlenen menfur saldırı nedeniyle taziyelerini iletirken, Türkiye’nin hangi dini gruba yönelik olursa olsun bu tür nefret suçlarına karşı olduğunu söyledi.

result.text:
Honored to visit the historic city of Kristianstad for productive talks with @MariaStenergard.

I expressed our deepest gratitude for Sweden’s leadership, with total assistance to Ukraine now exceeding $13.5 billion. From defense cooperation to energy assistance, Swedish solidarity continues to save lives and fortify our resilience.

Building on the dialogue between our leaders, President Zelenskyy and Prime Minister Kristersson, today we had excellent talks on expanding defense cooperation, the ‘Gripen’ jets, and Sweden’s support for our energy resilience. I thanked Sweden for its decisiveness. 

Ukraine is ready to share its unique combat, drone, and counter-drone experience to strengthen our Swedish partners and all of
Europe. We are mutually reinforcing each other.
 
We also discussed peace efforts and the new role of Europe, including Sweden’s contribution.  

I thanked Sweden for its steadfast support for our EU accession—backed by 91% of its citizens— including the opening of the first negotiation cluster this May and other five clusters already in June. 

Standing by the monument of Hetman Pylyp Orlyk and walking among the city’s chestnut trees, I truly felt the profound, centuries-old historical bonds that unite Ukraine and Sweden. 🇺🇦🇸🇪

The Russian-Chinese declaration signed by Putin and Xi contains the wording about removing the fundamental causes of the war in Ukraine - that is NATO expansion into ex-Soviet territory and Russia’s exclusion from European security arrangements.

On top of that, Xi spoke about threat of reviving militarism and fascism.

Doesn’t look like the goal of disuniting China and Russia is even remotely close to fruition.

result.text:
My colleague @rosenthal_jon looks at quiet Nordic/Baltic debates over what command & control structures might be usable if the US blocked NATO channels. The problem is that while JEF is agile & well suited to smaller crises in northern Europe, it's still a million miles away from being a suitable vehicle to co-ordinate large-scale collective defence in central/east Europe. We are still at the very early stages of these 'how to hedge' debates and the main effort is still on building up the so-called 'European pillar' within NATO, not outside it.

result.text:
I spoke with security policy officials from Lithuania, Latvia and Estonia 🇱🇹🇱🇻🇪🇪 about recent provocations, threats and drones. In short:

▪️Officials see no evidence of a sharply increased threat level or imminent Russian action. Russia currently lacks the capacity for that. But it is “raising the temperature” – hoping to spread fear, anxiety and uncertainty.

▪️The threats are viewed as an attempt to deter the Baltic states from supporting Ukraine and to divide them. They also reflect Moscow’s irritation over painful Ukrainian drone strikes and the broader course of the war, which has recently been unfavourable to the Kremlin.

▪️That does not make the references to specific sites in Latvia, or the Kaliningrad factor, irrelevant. Russia used similar pressure against Lithuania in 2022 over Kaliningrad–related sanctions. The answer remains the same: stronger national capabilities, better allied integration, and greater resilience.

result.text:
For centuries, Ukrainians have suffered occupation, wars, genocide, Stalin’s repressions, the Chornobyl disaster, and other tragedies driven from Moscow. Millions of lives were destroyed – yet those responsible were never held accountable. 

Eighty years after the Nuremberg Tribunal established that aggression is the supreme international crime, the world has a chance to act on that principle once again.

The Special Tribunal for the Crime of Aggression against Ukraine is about breaking a vicious cycle in which Russian violence against Ukraine came without consequences. It is a historic chance to restore justice that generations of Ukrainians never had.

There are many questions regarding the DS map at the moment, like Kryva Luka near Sloviansk, for example, and even Stipnohirsk where Ukrainian forces were allegedly advancing.
